name: shelf-write-roadmap-note
description: Mirror a single .kiln/vision.md or .kiln/roadmap/{phases,items}/*.md file into the Obsidian project subtree. Path composition reads .shelf-config directly ‚Äî NO vault discovery. FR-030 / FR-031 / FR-035 of structured-roadmap.

steps.instruction:
You are the `obsidian-write` step of the `shelf:shelf-write-roadmap-note` sub-workflow. Your job: write EXACTLY ONE Obsidian note for the roadmap source file that was just created or updated in `.kiln/`. No dashboard writes, no progress writes, no GitHub fetches, no manifest-scope writes, no other sub-workflow invocations.

## MCP scope

You may ONLY call:
- `mcp__claude_ai_obsidian-projects__create_file`
- `mcp__claude_ai_obsidian-projects__patch_file` (as the cold-start collision fallback AND as the patch path for subsequent writes ‚Äî see ¬ß3, ¬ß4)

You MUST NOT call: `list_files`, `append_file`, `update_file`, `read_file`, `search_files`, `batch_upsert`, or ANY `mcp__claude_ai_obsidian-manifest__*` tool. You MUST NOT invoke `shelf-propose-manifest-improvement`, `shelf-sync`, or any other workflow/skill.

## Step 1 ‚Äî Read inputs

From your context:

- `read-shelf-config` output: a fixed-shape structured block bounded by `## SHELF_CONFIG_PARSED` and `## END_SHELF_CONFIG_PARSED`. Between those markers are four lines:
  - `slug = <value or empty>`
  - `base_path = <value or empty>`
  - `dashboard_path = <value or empty>`
  - `shelf_config_present = <true|false>`
  Parse these four values verbatim. DO NOT call the parser or re-read `.shelf-config` yourself ‚Äî consume the structured block as-is. FR-004 invariant: this step is the SOLE reader of `.shelf-config`.
- `parse-roadmap-input` output: JSON blob `{ source_file, basename, frontmatter, body, obsidian_subpath }`. Parse it. If the JSON is malformed or `source_file` is empty OR `obsidian_subpath` is empty, write the error result in ¬ß5 and stop.

## Step 2 ‚Äî Compute target path + path_source (decision rule)

Apply this decision rule VERBATIM (mirrors `shelf-write-issue-note` ¬ßStep 2 ‚Äî only the subpath tail differs):

```
IF shelf_config_present == "true" AND slug != "" AND base_path != "":
  path_source = ".shelf-config (base_path + slug)"
  target_path = "${base_path}/${slug}/${obsidian_subpath}"
ELSE:
  path_source = "discovery (shelf-config incomplete)"
  slug      = $(basename of git remote, .git stripped) ; fallback to "$(basename $(pwd))"
  base_path = "projects"
  target_path = "${base_path}/${slug}/${obsidian_subpath}"
```

`path_source` is one of EXACTLY these two literal strings (no embellishments, no variations):
- `".shelf-config (base_path + slug)"`
- `"discovery (shelf-config incomplete)"`

The `discovery` branch MUST NOT call `mcp__claude_ai_obsidian-projects__list_files`. FR-004 of structured-roadmap: "discovery" here means "fall back to derived defaults" ‚Äî NOT vault listing.

`obsidian_subpath` is taken verbatim from parse-roadmap-input's output and is one of:
- `vision.md`
- `roadmap/phases/<basename>`
- `roadmap/items/<basename>`

Example success path: `second-brain/projects/ai-repo-template/roadmap/items/2026-04-24-batch-x.md`.

## Step 3 ‚Äî Compose the note content

Render the note based on `obsidian_subpath` shape:

### 3a ‚Äî `vision.md`

```
---
type: vision
project: "[[<slug>]]"
last_synced: <current ISO-8601 UTC timestamp>
---

<body from parse-roadmap-input ‚Äî verbatim>

*Synced from roadmap: `<source_file>`*
```

FR-031: vision updates on subsequent writes use `patch_file` (frontmatter-preserving).

### 3b ‚Äî `roadmap/phases/<basename>`

```
---
type: roadmap-phase
name: <frontmatter.name>
status: <frontmatter.status>
order: <frontmatter.order>
started: <frontmatter.started || null>
completed: <frontmatter.completed || null>
project: "[[<slug>]]"
source: roadmap:<basename>
last_synced: <current ISO-8601 UTC timestamp>
tags:
  - source/roadmap
  - phase/<frontmatter.name>
  - status/<frontmatter.status>
---

# <frontmatter.name> phase

<body from parse-roadmap-input ‚Äî verbatim>

*Synced from roadmap: `<source_file>`*
```

### 3c ‚Äî `roadmap/items/<basename>`

```
---
type: roadmap-item
id: <frontmatter.id>
title: <frontmatter.title>
kind: <frontmatter.kind>
state: <frontmatter.state>
status: <frontmatter.status>
phase: "[[<frontmatter.phase>]]"
date: <frontmatter.date>
blast_radius: <frontmatter.blast_radius>
review_cost: <frontmatter.review_cost>
context_cost: <frontmatter.context_cost>
project: "[[<slug>]]"
source: roadmap:<basename>
last_synced: <current ISO-8601 UTC timestamp>
tags:
  - source/roadmap
  - kind/<frontmatter.kind>
  - state/<frontmatter.state>
  - phase/<frontmatter.phase>
---

# <frontmatter.title>

<body from parse-roadmap-input ‚Äî verbatim>

*Synced from roadmap: `<source_file>`*
```

If `frontmatter.kind == "critique"`, ALSO include a `proof_path: <frontmatter.proof_path>` line in the composed frontmatter (between `context_cost` and `project`).

Optional roadmap-item keys (`depends_on`, `addresses`, `implementation_hints`, `prd`, `spec`): copy them through verbatim if present in source frontmatter ‚Äî position them after `phase:` and before `project:`.

## Step 4 ‚Äî Write via MCP (single call)

FR-031: action selection per file kind:

| `obsidian_subpath` shape | First write | Subsequent write |
|---|---|---|
| `vision.md`               | `create_file` | `patch_file` (frontmatter-preserving) |
| `roadmap/phases/<basename>` | `create_file` | `patch_file` |
| `roadmap/items/<basename>`  | `create_file` | `patch_file` |

Call `mcp__claude_ai_obsidian-projects__create_file({ path: "<target-path>", content: "<composed note>" })` exactly once.

### Cold-start collision fallback

If the create call fails with an "already exists" (or equivalent "file exists", HTTP 409, or path-collision) error, fall back to `mcp__claude_ai_obsidian-projects__patch_file({ path: "<target-path>", frontmatter: { source: "roadmap:<basename>", project: "[[<slug>]]", last_synced: "<now>", status: <frontmatter.status if present else null>, state: <frontmatter.state if present else null> } })`.

IMPORTANT on the patch fallback: DO NOT include `tags`, `type`, `kind`, `blast_radius`, `review_cost`, `context_cost`, `title`, `phase`, `date`, or body content. Those are INFERRED/derived fields set on creation only and must not be clobbered on an existing note. For `vision.md` patches, patch ONLY `last_synced` + `project`.

Set result action = `"created"` on a successful create, `"patched"` on a successful patch fallback.

## Step 5 ‚Äî Emit result

Write EXACTLY ONE JSON object to your output file `.wheel/outputs/shelf-write-roadmap-note-result.json`:

Success:
```json
{
  "source_file": "<the .kiln/... path from context>",
  "obsidian_path": "<target path you wrote to>",
  "action": "created" | "patched",
  "path_source": ".shelf-config (base_path + slug)" | "discovery (shelf-config incomplete)",
  "errors": []
}
```

Error (parse failure / all MCP calls fail):
```json
{
  "source_file": "<path or empty>",
  "obsidian_path": "<target or empty>",
  "action": "failed",
  "path_source": "unknown",
  "errors": ["<short diagnostic>"]
}
```

`path_source` MUST be present in every result JSON ‚Äî one of the three literal strings above (`.shelf-config (base_path + slug)`, `discovery (shelf-config incomplete)`, or `unknown` on failure). No variations.

An error result MUST NOT crash the sub-workflow ‚Äî `action: "failed"` is returned normally and the caller decides what to do.

## Invariants

- At most ONE successful MCP write per run (either one create OR one patch ‚Äî never both).
- No other tool calls after the write + result emission.
- If `source_file` cannot be determined, the step still emits the error result (with `path_source: "unknown"`) and exits normally.
- The decision rule for `path_source` uses the structured block from `read-shelf-config` VERBATIM. Do not re-parse `.shelf-config` directly.
- The `discovery` branch MUST NOT call `list_files` ‚Äî FR-004 compliance.

steps.command: RESULT=".wheel/outputs/shelf-write-roadmap-note-result.json"; if [ ! -s "$RESULT" ] || ! jq -e . "$RESULT" >/dev/null 2>&1; then printf '{"source_file":"","obsidian_path":"","action":"failed","path_source":"unknown","errors":["obsidian-write agent produced empty or invalid result JSON"]}\n' > "$RESULT"; fi; echo '## shelf-write-roadmap-note result'; jq . "$RESULT"
